step.title: Create Step Files
step.goal: Generate the individual step files based on the workflow design. Each step file contains detailed instructions, guidance, and examples.
step.note: This step only applies to workflow-type skills. We create one XML file per step in references/steps/.

substep.title: Generate Step Files
substep.action:
Create step file: .vera/skills/{{skill_id}}/references/steps/step-NN-{{slug}}.xml (where NN is zero-padded step number and slug is the step's kebab-case summary)
Each step file follows the structure: 1. step-file wrapper with skill, step, title attributes 2. step element with goal, substeps, actions 3. guidance section with rules and examples 4. examples section with concrete configurations

substep.title: Add Guidance Sections
substep.action:
For each step file, add relevant guidance:
- What decisions are made in this step? - What are the rules/constraints? - What are good vs bad examples? - What common mistakes should be avoided?
Guidance makes the skill self-documenting and helps the LLM make better decisions.

substep.title: Add Examples
substep.action:
Add 2-3 concrete examples per step:
- Example name and context - Complete config/output for that example - Rationale explaining WHY this is a good example
Examples ground the instructions in reality.

substep.title: Review Step Files
substep.template-output:
List created step files with per-file summary (filename, goal, substep count, presence of guidance and examples).
[a] Review/edit specific files [c] Continue [y] YOLO

section.title: Step File Structure
section.description: Every step file follows this XML structure:
section.template:
```xml <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <step-file skill="skill-name" step="N" title="Step Title">
<step n="N" title="Step Title"> <goal>What this step accomplishes</goal>
<substep n="Na" title="First Substep"> <action>What to do</action> <ask>Question for user</ask> </substep>
<substep n="Nb" title="Second Substep"> <template-output section="Section Name"> Content to save... [a] Adjust [c] Continue [y] YOLO </template-output> </substep> </step>
<guidance> <section title="Topic"> <rules><rule>Rule 1</rule></rules> <example type="good">Good example</example> <example type="bad">Bad example</example> </section> </guidance>
<examples> <example name="Example Name"> <context>When this applies</context> <config>The actual configuration</config> <rationale>Why this is good</rationale> </example> </examples>
</step-file> ```

section.title: Writing Good Guidance

rules.rule: Focus on decisions made in THIS step
Include both rules (what to do) and anti-patterns (what not to do)
Use good/bad example pairs to illustrate rules

section.example: "Skill names must be kebab-case and start with a verb" Good: create-product-brief Bad: ProductBrief (wrong case)
"Names should be good" (too vague, not actionable)

rules.rule: Include context: when/why this example applies
Always include rationale: WHY is this good?
Show variety: different types, edge cases
Each substep should do ONE thing
Use ask for user input (ALWAYS waits for response)
Substeps numbered as Na, Nb, Nc...
slug is kebab-case summary: discovery, scaffold, validate

example.name: Discovery Step
example.config:
<step n="1" title="Discovery & Context"> <goal>Understand what the user wants to create</goal>
<substep n="1a" title="Gather Requirements"> <ask>What would you like to create? Please describe...</ask> </substep>
<substep n="1b" title="Confirm Understanding"> <template-output section="Discovery Summary"> Summary of requirements... [a] Explore more [c] Continue </template-output> </substep> </step>
example.rationale: Standard discovery pattern: ask → confirm. Template-output creates checkpoint for user to verify understanding.

example.name: Validation Step
example.config:
<step n="8" title="Validate & Complete"> <goal>Run validation and present next steps</goal>
<substep n="8a" title="Run Validation"> <action type="script">uv run scripts/validate.py {{output_file}}</action> <check if="validation fails"> <action>Fix issues and re-run</action> </check> </substep>
<substep n="8b" title="Present Summary"> <template-output section="Completion Summary" critical="true"> ## Created Successfully Location: ... Next steps: ... </template-output> </substep> </step>
example.rationale: Standard completion pattern: validate → summarize. Script runs validation, summary shows what was created and next steps.
